mission.title: valkey: [NEW] Negative filtering for client command filters
mission.description: [NEW] Negative filtering for client command filters. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current valkey setup
steps.description:
Verify your valkey version and configuration:
```bash
valkey-server --version
```
This feature requires a working valkey installation.

steps.title: Review valkey configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant valkey configuration:
does this PR support negative filtering?

_Originally posted by  in https://github.com/valkey-io/valkey/issues/1466#issuecomment-2647089232_

steps.title: Apply the fix for [NEW] Negative filtering for client command filters
steps.description:
introduce negative filters for `CLIENT LIST` and `CLIENT KILL` commands:

1. `NOT-ID`: Excludes clients in the IDs set
2. `NOT-TYPE`: Excludes clients of the specified type
3. `NOT-ADDR`: Excludes clients of the specified address and port
4. `NOT-LADDR`: Excludes clients connected to the specified
```yaml
127.0.0.1:6379> client kill ADDR ~ 127.0.0.1:23456  // Kill all clients except 127.0.0.1:23456
127.0.0.1:6379> client kill TYPE ~ NORMAL  // Kill all clients except NORMAL type
127.0.0.1:6379> client kill USER ~ user1  // Kill all clients except user1
127.0.0.1:6379> client kill NAME ~ valkey  // Kill all clients except named valkey
127.0.0.1:6379> client kill SKIPME YES/NO // Do not need filtering
127.0.0.1:6379> client kill ID ~ client-id-1 client-id-2  // Kill all clients except id-1 and id-2
127.0.0.1:6379> client kill MAXAGE maxage // Do not need filtering, but we can add new option like:
```

steps.title: Upgrade valkey to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade valkey:
```bash
# Check current version
valkey-server --version
# Follow the project's upgrade guide at https://github.com/valkey-io/valkey
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "[NEW] Negative filtering for client command filters" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
introduce negative filters for `CLIENT LIST` and `CLIENT KILL` commands:

1. `NOT-ID`: Excludes clients in the IDs set
2. `NOT-TYPE`: Excludes clients of the specified type
3. `NOT-ADDR`: Excludes clients of the specified address and port
4. `NOT-LADDR`: Excludes clients connected to the specified local address and port
5. `NOT-USER`: Excludes clients of the specified user
6.
codeSnippets:
127.0.0.1:6379> client kill ADDR ~ 127.0.0.1:23456  // Kill all clients except 127.0.0.1:23456
127.0.0.1:6379> client kill TYPE ~ NORMAL  // Kill all clients except NORMAL type
127.0.0.1:6379> client kill USER ~ user1  // Kill all clients except user1
127.0.0.1:6379> client kill NAME ~ valkey  // Kill all clients except named valkey
127.0.0.1:6379> client kill SKIPME YES/NO // Do not need filtering
127.0.0.1:6379> client kill ID ~ client-id-1 client-id-2  // Kill all clients except id-1 and id-2
127.0.0.1:6379> client kill MAXAGE maxage // Do not need filtering, but we can add new option like: client kill AGE >10|<=600
127.0.0.1:6379> client kill FLAGS ~ flag // Kill all clients except flag

CLIENT LIST [ TYPE normal | master | replica | pubsub ] [ ID client-id ] [ [ ID client-id ] ... ] [ USER username ] [ NOT-USER username ] [ ADDR addr ] [ NOT-ADDR addr ] [ LADDR laddr ] [ SKIPME YES | NO ] [ MAXAGE maxage ] [ NAME name ] [ IDLE idle ] [ FLAGS flags ] [ LIB-NAME lib-name ] [ LIB-VER lib-ver ] [ DB db ] [ CAPA capa ] [ IP ip ]

prerequisites.description: A working valkey installation or development environment.
